key_insight: EasyNano enables fast, epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design by backpropagating through ESMFold2 distogram predictions instead of relying on slow stochastic sampling.
engineering_relevance: This gives protein engineers a practical workstation-scale workflow for rapidly prototyping binding-specific nanobodies for therapeutic and diagnostic targets.
method: Gradient-descent optimization of CDR residue logits through the ESMFold2-Fast pairwise distance distogram
Composite loss includes an epitope proximity term to steer binding toward a user-specified target site
Full ESMFold2 CA-coordinate priors help keep the nanobody framework pose stable during design
evidence_signal: Across six target-framework cases, EasyNano improved ipTM by up to 0.559 and outperformed random CDR baselines with strong statistical significance, including a 5.7-sigma gain on Ty1.
watch_item: Performance depends on practical choices such as wild-type logit initialization and multi-seed replication, since optimization can converge to different local minima.
paper_title: EasyNano: rapid epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design via differentiable distogram optimization with ESMFold2
